authors: Stauss T, El Majdoub F, Sayed D, Surges G, Rosenberg WS, Kapural L, Bundschu R, Lalkhen A, Patel N, Gliner B, Subbaroyan J, Rotte A, Edgar DR, Bettag M, Maarouf M
title: A multicenter real-world review of 10 kHz SCS outcomes for treatment of chronic trunk and/or limb pain
journal_name: Annals of Clinical and Translational Neurology
study_question: Is 10 kHz SCS effective for chronic trunk and/or limb pain in real-world practice?
population_assessed: 1660 patients with chronic trunk and/or limb pain trialed or implanted with 10 kHz SCS.
follow_up_duration: 3, 6, 12 months post-implantation; mean 8.9 months to last visit
outcome_measures: Responder rate, pain intensity, function, sleep, quality of life, medication intake
inclusion_criteria: Chronic trunk and/or limb pain; trialed or implanted with 10 kHz SCS, April 2014-January 2018.
answer_to_study_question: Over 70% had sustained pain relief, with improved function, sleep, and quality of life.
reasoning: Bibliographic fields were unanimous across all models. Study design conflicted, but two models chose retrospective_cohort_controlled, including one with higher confidence than the uncontrolled classification.
field_level_decisions_json: {"study_design": "Preferred retrospective_cohort_controlled over case_series_uncontrolled based on majority and higher confidence.", "follow_up_duration": "Merged shared 3, 6, 12 month schedule with mean 8.9 month last visit.", "outcome_measures": "Used fuller consensus list including pain intensity from Anthropic."}
